summary.title: Brew & Bloom Coffee Co. Ordering Application
summary.description: The Brew & Bloom Coffee Co. ordering web application is a digital platform designed to enhance the customer experience and streamline internal operations of the coffee shop. It provides a convenient online platform for customers to browse the menu, customize items, and place orders for pickup or delivery, while equipping staff with tools for efficient order and menu management. The architecture is composed of four key components: the Client Side (Frontend), the Backend Web Application Server, the Database Server, and an integrated Third-Party Payment Service.

diagrams.title: Customer Places an Order

threats.title: Customer Account Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker could impersonate a legitimate customer to place fraudulent orders or access their order history.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Web Application Server Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker might set up a fraudulent web server to phish user credentials or intercept sensitive data.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Order Data Tampering
threats.description: An attacker could modify order details (e.g., items, quantities, prices, status) in transit or at rest.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Fraudulent Order Repudiation
threats.description: A customer could deny placing an order if insufficient logging and non-repudiation controls are in place.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Customer PII Disclosure
threats.description: Unauthorized access to customer names, contact details, delivery addresses, and order history.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Web Application Server DoS
threats.description: An attacker could flood the Web Application Server with excessive requests, making it unavailable.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Customer to Staff Privilege Escalation
threats.description: A customer could exploit a vulnerability to gain unauthorized access to Barista, Manager, or Administrator functionalities.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: New order details and payment status

labels: New order details and payment status

diagrams.description: The customer selects and customizes items, submitting the order details to the Web Application Server. The server performs validation and initiates a payment request to the Third-Party Payment Gateway. Once the payment is successfully processed, the server records the new order and payment status in the MongoDB Database and sends an order confirmation back to the customer.

data.name: Login and request to view incoming orders

labels: Login and request to view incoming orders

data.name: Update order record with validated status

labels: Update order record with validated status

diagrams.description: The barista logs into the staff interface to view incoming orders. After the Web Application Server authenticates the barista, it retrieves pending order data from the MongoDB Database and displays it to the barista. The barista then selects an order, updates its status, and the server validates this update before saving the new status in the database.

data.name: Login and request to view basic sales reports

labels: Login and request to view basic sales reports

data.name: Process and send basic sales report for display

labels: Process and send basic sales report for display

diagrams.description: The manager logs into the staff interface and requests basic sales reports from the Web Application Server. The server authenticates the manager and queries the MongoDB Database for aggregated sales data. The database returns this data to the server, which processes and formats it before sending the report to the manager's browser for display

data.name: Input new menu item details and submit

labels: Input new menu item details and submit

data.name: Send new menu item data for creation

labels: Send new menu item data for creation

data.name: Confirmation of created menu item record

labels: Confirmation of created menu item record

data.name: Send confirmation of successful menu item addition

labels: Send confirmation of successful menu item addition

diagrams.description: The administrator logs into the staff interface and submits the details for a new menu item, such as its name, description, and price. The Web Application Server receives this data, performs input validation, and checks the administrator's authorization. It then sends the data to the MongoDB Database to create the new menu item record and returns a success confirmation to the administrator.
